tech.label: Tech, AI & Cybersecurity
tech.briefing: Today's Tech, AI & Cybersecurity section covers 30 stories.

articles.title: New court filing reveals Pentagon told Anthropic the two sides were nearly aligned — a week after Trump declared the relationship kaput
articles.summary: Anthropic submitted two sworn declarations to a California federal court late Friday afternoon, pushing back on the Pentagon's assertion that the AI company poses an "unacceptable risk to national security" and arguing that the government's case relies on technical misunderstandi

articles.title: Anthropic Denies It Could Sabotage AI Tools During War
articles.summary: The Department of Defense alleges the AI developer could manipulate models in the middle of war. Company executives argue that’s impossible.

articles.title: Jury finds Elon Musk s stupid tweets caused Twitter investors losses
articles.summary: A California jury determined that Elon Musk misled Twitter investors before making a $44 billion deal to buy the company in 2022, reports CNBC . The New York Times reports that Musk had testified this month that he didn't believe his posts would spook markets, but he did say that

articles.title: Elon Musk misled Twitter investors while trying to get out of acquisition, jury says
articles.summary: At the time, Musk had tweeted that Twitter had too many bots, which is why he was trying to renege on his commitment to buy the company.

articles.title: Jury finds Musk owes damages to Twitter investors for his tweets
articles.summary: On Friday, a jury in California determined that Elon Musk had misled investors in Twitter via public statements that depressed the price of the company's stock ahead of Musk's purchase of the service. Because this was a class action lawsuit, Musk is likely to owe damages to a hug

articles.title: You're likely already infected with a brain-eating virus you've never heard of
articles.summary: There's a virus you may have never heard of before that is estimated to infect up to 90 percent of people and lurks quietly in your cells for life—but if it becomes activated, it will destroy your brain. If that's not startling enough, researchers reported this week that there ma

articles.title: There Aren’t a Lot of Reasons to Get Excited About a New Amazon Smartphone
articles.summary: The company is reportedly building a new AI-powered mobile device. If Amazon follows through on the plan, experts warn it would be next to impossible to break into a crowded market.

articles.title: An automated moderation error left Tumblr users panicked
articles.summary: Tumblr users were left scrambling on Wednesday after dozens of accounts were banned in the same afternoon by an automated system. Numerous users contacted The Verge about the incident, claiming that the wave of bans disproportionately seemed to impact accounts run by users who id

articles.title: Once again, ULA can't deliver when the US military needs a satellite in orbit
articles.summary: For the fourth time in a little more than a year, the US Space Force needs to send up a new satellite to replenish the military's GPS navigation network. And once again, the company the Pentagon is paying to launch it can't answer the call. United Launch Alliance, a 50-50 joint v

articles.title: Microsoft keeps insisting that it's deeply committed to the quality of Windows 11
articles.summary: If you were eating in a restaurant and the head chef came out from the back multiple times to loudly proclaim that the kitchen was deeply committed to the quality of the food, would you find that reassuring? Or would you start wondering why the chef felt the need to keep saying i

articles.title: ‘A Rigged and Dangerous Product’: The Wildest Week for Prediction Markets Yet
articles.summary: As the prediction market boom continues, backlash is growing, too, with Arizona filing criminal charges against Kalshi and public outcry after Polymarket traders threatened a journalist.

articles.title: Writer denies it, but publisher pulls horror novel after multiple allegations of AI use
articles.summary: Shy Girl , a horror novel by Mia Ballard, was one of those buzzy books that leapt from self-published prominence into full-on trade publication. Until yesterday, that is, when publisher Hachette pulled the book from the UK market and canceled plans to bring it to the US. The move

articles.title: Microsoft rolls back some of its Copilot AI bloat on Windows
articles.summary: The company is reducing Copilot entry points on Windows, starting with Photos, Widgets, Notepad, and other apps.

articles.title: Widely used Trivy scanner compromised in ongoing supply-chain attack
articles.summary: Hackers have compromised virtually all versions of Aqua Security’s widely used Trivy vulnerability scanner in an ongoing supply chain attack that could have wide-ranging consequences for developers and the organizations that use them. Trivy maintainer Itay Shakury confirmed the c

articles.title: A Top Democrat Is Urging Colleagues to Support Trump’s Spy Machine
articles.summary: Congressman Jim Himes claims a sweeping surveillance authority should stay intact because he hasn't seen abuses by Kash Patel's FBI, according to internal messaging obtained by WIRED.

articles.title: FBI links Signal phishing attacks to Russian intelligence services
articles.summary: The FBI has issued a public service announcement warning that Russian intelligence-linked threat actors are actively targeting users of encrypted messaging apps such as Signal and WhatsApp in phishing campaigns that have already compromised thousands of accounts. [...]

articles.title: NASA issues draft request for moving space shuttle Discovery—or Orion capsule
articles.summary: NASA has taken a step forward to moving an undetermined spacecraft of a various size on an indefinite date to a yet-to-be-decided location. Or to put it another way: NASA is seeking to learn more about what it would take to remove the space shuttle Discovery from the Smithsonian

articles.title: Trump FCC lets Nexstar buy Tegna and blow way past 39% TV ownership cap
articles.summary: The Federal Communications Commission yesterday approved Nexstar Media Group's $6.2 billion purchase of Tegna, granting a waiver that lets the broadcast giant go way past the national limit on station ownership. Nexstar said it closed the acquisition late in the day yesterday, im

articles.title: What happened at Nvidia GTC: NemoClaw, Robot Olaf, and a $1 trillion bet
articles.summary: CEO Jensen Huang took the stage at Nvidia s GTC conference this week in his signature leather jacket to deliver a two-and-a-half-hour keynote, projecting $1 trillion in AI chip sales through 2027, declaring that every company needs an “OpenClaw strategy,” and closing with a rambl

articles.title: A new Nintendo Switch 2 could be the poster child for replaceable batteries
articles.summary: Nintendo plans to release a Switch 2 revision in the European Union that will let users replace their own batteries, Nikkei reports . The current version of the Switch 2 has a glued-in battery. But Nintendo apparently plans to make the change in order to comply with EU rules goin

articles.title: Future Sony PlayStation games will use AI to imagine new frames
articles.summary: Mark Cerny, the lead architect of the PlayStation 5 and PS5 Pro, told Digital Foundry that ML-based frame generation tech is coming to "PlayStation platforms" in the future, letting the game console use AI to imagine new frames between the ones it's actually rendering, which can

articles.title: Patch Now: Oracle's Fusion Middleware Has Critical RCE Flaw
articles.summary: Attackers can execute arbitrary code without authentication if Oracle's Identity or Web Services Managers are exposed to the Web.

articles.title: Amid legal turmoil, Kalshi is temporarily banned in Nevada
articles.summary: It's the latest escalation in a regulatory battle over the future of prediction markets.

articles.title: Gamers Hate Nvidia's DLSS 5. Developers Aren’t Crazy About It, Either
articles.summary: Nvidia’s new AI upscaling gaming technology struck gamers as uncanny and off-putting. Developers don't seem to like it, either, but it could be “the default” in a few years.

articles.title: Pinterest CEO calls on governments to ban social media for users under 16
articles.summary: Pinterest’s chief compares social media to tobacco and alcohol, saying children need similar protections.

articles.title: Windows 11 is finally getting a movable taskbar
articles.summary: It's taken almost five years, but Microsoft is finally bringing back the ability to move the Windows taskbar to the top or sides of your screen. The movable taskbar is part of some much bigger changes coming to Windows 11 this year in response to growing criticism of the operatin

articles.title: Microsoft is ending the Windows Update nightmare — and letting you pause them indefinitely
articles.summary: In 2015, Microsoft decided that you shouldn't be in control of updating your PC anymore. At first, it seemed like a good idea to keep malware at bay - but soon, users discovered their computers were automatically shutting down and erasing work in the middle of the day. Then, Micr

articles.title: This is Microsoft s plan to fix Windows 11
articles.summary: Microsoft has faced a breakdown of trust in Windows 11 and a backlash over AI additions to its operating system in recent months. After promising to rebuild trust in Windows earlier this year, Microsoft's Windows chief, Pavan Davuluri, is now revealing the company's plan to fix W

articles.title: Oracle pushes emergency fix for critical Identity Manager RCE flaw
articles.summary: Oracle has released an out-of-band security update to fix a critical unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in Identity Manager and Web Services Manager tracked as CVE-2026-21992. [...]

articles.title: Trump takes another shot at dismantling state AI regulation

articles.title: Malaysia Has Not Informed US of Withdrawal From Trade Deal: Official
